Creating space for meaningful questions is often the first step toward real change.

That’s why Wolfgang Toechterle, CMO & Member of the Board IDM Südtirol-Alto Adige, was not only part of this year’s BEAM Summit, but with IDM Südtirol-Alto Adige also one of our main partners.

For Wolfgang, transformation doesn’t happen on paper. It happens in collaboration — through real action, shared values, and powerful partnerships.

Together with partners like IDM, BEAM creates a space where ideas, perspectives and collaboration drive meaningful change.

Food sits at the intersection of health, agriculture, economy and culture. And hospitality is where these worlds meet every day.

Christian Hamerle invites us to rethink the role of food and hospitality as catalysts for healthier people, stronger ecosystems and a more sustainable economy.

This is exactly the kind of systemic thinking that BEAM brings to the conversation on the future of hospitality.

It was a pleasure having Food Service Innovation Lab and Dussmann Group as partner again this year.

Over the past days, collaboration has proven to be more than just a theme, it has been a concrete way of working, exchanging ideas, and rethinking the future of the industry together.

Because it is only by connecting perspectives and turning insights into action that hospitality can truly evolve toward a more human, sustainable, and forward-looking direction.

One question running through everything: 
what does it actually take to lead the shift in hospitality?

Be Ambitious.
And see you at BEAM 2027 :)

Making #BEAM, we didn't organise a conference.
We led a shift.
Two days in Bolzano.
Close to 400 game changers. 35+ speakers. 40+ partners.

Labs, keynotes, and connections designed to outlast the event.

But here's what the numbers don't show:
The conversations that started over lunch and became partnerships by dinner.
The speaker who said something that changed how a room full of people sees their own industry.
The moment you realise you're not alone in believing hospitality can be more.

More human. More sustainable. More transformative.

The future doesn't happen. It's built.

Thank you to every speaker, partner, participant, and collaborator who showed up, challenged the status quo, and chose to lead.

The shift has been led :)

You've optimized your menu, your team, your brand.

But have you ever designed what a guest feels the moment they walk in?

I FEEL SPACE is the BEAM Lab exploring light, acoustics, and haptic design as strategic tools for guest experience — with BIG - Bjarke Ingels Group, Grau GmbH, and Vitra.

The lab will be held by: Robert Grimm, Timon and Melchior Grau, Corina Meroth, Joana D. and Philipp Kirnbauer.

💡 The workshop outcomes will be co-authored by curators Joana D. and Philipp Kirnbauer, with Professor Meng-Mei (Maggie) Chen from EHL, and used as a curriculum case study.

50+ national tourism associations in Germany. 
50 presidents. 50 agendas. 50 different voices.
Result? Zero political relevance.
Michael Otremba called it out at BEAM: "During COVID, the transport minister met with the automotive industry every week. With tourism? Never."
Travel is a primal human aspiration. The first books ever written were travel reports. The Odyssey itself is one long journey.
Yet as an industry, we're invisible. 
Why? Because we can't agree on anything except overnight numbers.
"I understand every minister who says: clarify your opinion first, then come back to me." 
Seven years later, nothing happens.
It's time to stop being comfortable.

BEAM 2026 | May 19-20 | Bolzano

9,000 residents. 4.5 million visitors.
Immense traffic. Trail degradation. Human-wildlife conflicts.
Banff National Park was breaking.

Leslie Bruce ICD.D, CDME did something radical: she invited their biggest regulators and harshest critics to build the future together.

Federal government, local government, and business community at the same table. 

2,000 people surveyed. 550+ ideas generated.

The result?
2M+ people on mass transit. Indigenous showcases supported. A community that went from adversaries to allies.

The secret?
Finding the one thing everyone agreed on: we all love this park.

See you at BEAM 2026? 
May 19-20 | Bolzano

Everyone talks about real transformation.
But what does it really mean in hospitality?

Boris Igorevic Lebedev (Speaker, Business Consultant, transformative facilitator) is one of the speakers at BEAM 2026 – and he’ll explore what a regenerative hospitality economy is…and what it isn’t.

REAL transformation is…
 Not superficial change.
 Not small adjustments.
 Not just marketing.

It’s an energetic stance.
 A different way of relating to life.
 A different way of doing business.
 Painful, risky – AND worth it.

With over 10 years in the industry and roles in Reinventing Society and Club of Rome Germany, Boris brings a clear perspective on how hospitality can move towards a regenerative future.

At BEAM, discover what that shift could look like.

We're consuming 1.7 Earths. By 2050, it'll be three planets.

Diana Verde Nieto didn't come to BEAM to depress us.
She came to show us the way forward.

Hospitality represents 10% of global GDP and touches every system: food, energy, culture, community, wellness. When you change hospitality, the ripple effect is massive.

But sustainability isn't enough anymore.
We need regeneration.

Her work in Tismoudin, Morocco, transformed an abandoned village into a thriving community in three years. 

How? By asking one question: "What are your dreams?"

Intention matters.

Wellness hotel. Coworking space. Community hub.

What if you didn't have to choose?

Magnus Busch (Founder nook society) is one of the speakers at BEAM 2026.

And he created something that didn't exist before: EXPERIENCE TRAVEL, a new segment in hospitality that combines wellness, new work, and community in a single business model.

But Magnus isn't just a visionary.
He's an operator who transforms underperforming properties into sought-after destinations through asset-light models, strategic repositioning, and innovative targeting.

From boutique hotels to wellness retreats, from member-managed spaces to places where culture and social life meet – nook society proves that hospitality can be reinvented.

At BEAM, he shares how he created a market from scratch and made properties profitable that others would have sold.

"Medical prescription: 7 days in Sweden"

Sweden is the first destination in the world to promote stays on medical prescription.

Not a slogan. Scientifically proven effects on physical and mental health.

At BEAM, discover how bold marketing generates results that best practices will never reach.

In the middle of Day 2 at BEAM 2025, something unexpected happened.

300 hospitality leaders stopped talking about strategy and KPIs. 
They stood up, closed their eyes, and moved their bodies.

For 10 minutes, Miriam Kirchler led them in somatic movement.

"The greatest collaboration you'll ever have in your entire life is the collaboration with yourself. Because you'll have it until your last breath."

Before you can lead a team, transform a business, or collaborate radically with others, you must first listen to yourself.

10 minutes. That's all it takes to remember.

Medieval monasteries. MIT Lab. Berghain. Salk Institute.

They all use the same architectural pattern.
A community center where exchange happens + individual periphery where concentration happens.

From I to WE. From WE to I.

This form has worked for a thousand years. 
And you can apply it to your hotel, restaurant, or event space.

At BEAM 2025, Philipp Kirnbauer and Christian Mueller showed us how to physically design and create spaces for successful interaction and collaboration. 

Creating spaces that work isn't complex. It's intentional.

BEAM 2026 | May 19-20 | Bolzano

Every week in Europe there are dozens of #hospitality events.

Most follow the same pattern: Chaotic registration. 
Stage keynotes. Standing lunch. Random #networking. Done.

#BEAM works differently.

We've engineered an event where every moment has a purpose.

WELCOME & FIRST CONNECTIONS 
Not just a check-in. The first moment of immersion. #Connections start before the event officially begins.

LUNCH EXPERIENCE 
Nobody eats while scrolling their phone. Tables designed to seat you next to someone who's solved the problem you're facing. The food is excellent. But the conversations are the real main course.

OPENING KEYNOTE + STRUCTURED NETWORKING 
Speakers who have failed, scaled, and are building right now. Then facilitated networking. Zero small talk. Maximum substance.

WORKSHOP SESSIONS 
Enough with colorful post-its and empty #brainstorming. Here you work on YOUR revenue model, YOUR #sustainability strategy, YOUR #digitalization process. With those who've already done it. And with those doing it better than you.

THE EVENING EXPERIENCE 
Unique South Tyrol location. Experiential dinner. Live entertainment. Zero formalities. Zero masks. Zero business cards on the table. This is where collaborations you never imagined are born.

AFTER BEAM 
The connections born here aren't the "let's stay in touch" kind. They're collaborations that continue. #Partnerships that develop. Calls when you really need perspective.

BEAM isn't just an event. It's where lasting relationships begin.

May 19-20, 2026 | Bolzano

Face-to-face interactions have dropped by 30% in the last decade. 

Teens don't go out anymore. They stay home.

We're living in what Philipp Kirnbauer calls the "Anti-Social Century", an era where we've lost the Circle of Village. 

That space where you meet people who disagree with you. 

Where you learn tolerance. Where serendipity happens.

Technology amplifies the I, not the WE.

And nothing truly wonderful will ever happen to you on a screen.

The solution? 
It's not another app. It's physical. Spatial. Human.

At BEAM 2025, we explored how to design spaces that rebuild the connections we've lost. Spaces that create the conditions for the Circle of Village to exist again.

Because in 2026, people don't choose who serves them better. 
They choose who makes them feel something.

BEAM 2026 | May 19-20 | Bolzano

BEAM 2026's final schedule is still being finalized, but one thing is certain: it won't be like any other event.

Day 1 → Opening Keynote, No-filter Panels, Intensive BEAM Labs, Exclusive Evening Experience

Hospitality is shifting fast.

But the real question isn’t what’s changing.

It’s this: will we spend the next years adapting… or leading the shift?

In a world of constant pressure (sustainability, digitalisation, new business models, new guest expectations), it’s easy to fall into “conference mode”: watch, take notes, go back to business as usual.

BEAM is built to do the opposite.

That’s why we designed it around 5 principles:

EXPERIENCE → immersive moments that change perspective.
EXCHANGE → ideas, tools and practices you can apply now.
CONNECT → engineered connections with the right people.
ACT → from vision to action, every moment moves you forward.
COMMUNITY → beyond an event: a year-round movement of game changers.

Because the future of hospitality doesn’t “happen”.

It gets built by the people who show up, share, test, and move together.

If this is the kind of hospitality you want to be part of, you already know where to be:
